report.executive_summary:
MEMORANDUM TO FILE - TITLE ANALYSIS AND CLOSING READINESS ASSESSMENT

RE: 675 Bunker Hill Road, Columbia, Marion County, Mississippi
Commitment No.: 01-26006219-01T | Commitment Date: January 16, 2026
Proposed Insured (Owner): Kyla B. Fortenberry | Proposed Insured (Lender): Society Mortgage
Underwriter: Old Republic National Title Insurance Company

CRITICAL STATUS: TRANSACTION BLOCKED - IMMEDIATE CORRECTIVE ACTION REQUIRED

This transaction is currently BLOCKED from closing due to a critical deed recording defect that prevents issuance of the owner's title policy. The recorded warranty deed (Instrument #20260174, recorded 01/13/2026) names Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson as grantees instead of the intended buyer, Kyla B. Fortenberry. This constitutes a complete failure to satisfy Requirement 4 and creates a material title defect that must be corrected through a new recorded deed before the transaction can proceed.

Additionally, significant issues exist regarding: (1) duplicate and overlapping mineral reservation exceptions requiring clarification; (2) a discrepancy between the vested owner (GARNER TREE FARM, LLC) and the listed seller (TERRI G. CRAWLEY); and (3) incomplete LLC compliance documentation. The current readiness score of 67/100 reflects these substantial deficiencies.

RECOMMENDATION: Do not proceed to closing until the deed defect is corrected, mineral exceptions are consolidated, the owner/seller discrepancy is resolved, and complete LLC documentation is submitted and reviewed.

  • Terri Garner Crawley
  • Patsy Garner Brewton
  • Karen Garner Delancey

CRITICAL PARTY ISSUE: The recorded deed names Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson as grantees, not Kyla B. Fortenberry. This creates a fundamental defect in the chain of title and prevents the intended buyer from obtaining clear title. The relationship between these named grantees and the intended buyer must be immediately clarified, and corrective action must be taken.
report.requirements:
SCHEDULE B, PART I - REQUIREMENTS STATUS

Requirement 1: Vested Owner/Seller Discrepancy Resolution
  • Description: Vested Owner is GARNER TREE FARM, LLC; Seller is TERRI G. CRAWLEY. Advise customer service of circumstances explaining discrepancy.
  • Status: OPEN - UNRESOLVED
  • Analysis: The commitment identifies GARNER TREE FARM, LLC as the vested owner with record title, yet TERRI G. CRAWLEY is listed as the seller. Terri Garner Crawley is identified as a member of GARNER TREE FARM, LLC. This discrepancy must be resolved to establish proper authority for the transaction. It is unclear whether TERRI G. CRAWLEY is acting as an individual member or as an authorized representative of the LLC. This must be clarified in writing with supporting documentation.

Requirement 2: Verification of Open Deed of Trust
  • Description: No open deed of trust found in Marion County records. Verify by inquiry of escrow personnel and/or proposed insured.
  • Status: OPEN - PENDING VERIFICATION
  • Analysis: Title search has not revealed any open deed of trust or mortgage lien against the property in Marion County records. However, verification must be obtained from escrow personnel and the proposed insured (Kyla B. Fortenberry) to confirm no other liens exist outside the county records or in other jurisdictions.

Requirement 3: Bankruptcy Search
  • Description: No bankruptcy proceedings found for GARNER TREE FARM, LLC, TERRI G. CRAWLEY, and KYLA B. FORTENBERRY.
  • Status: OPEN - PENDING COMPLETION
  • Analysis: Bankruptcy searches must be completed and certified for all three parties to ensure no bankruptcy proceedings exist that would affect title or the transaction. Federal bankruptcy records must be searched in the appropriate district court(s).

Requirement 4: Record Deed from GARNER TREE FARM, LLC to KYLA B. FORTENBERRY
  • Description: Record satisfactory executed Deed from GARNER TREE FARM, LLC, grantor(s) to KYLA B. FORTENBERRY, grantee(s), conveying the caption premises in Marion County records.
  • Status: CRITICAL - NOT SATISFIED / DEFECTIVE RECORDING
  • Analysis: THIS IS THE CRITICAL BLOCKING ISSUE. A deed was recorded on 01/13/2026 (Instrument #20260174) from GARNER TREE FARM, LLC as grantor; however, the grantees are listed as Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson, NOT Kyla B. Fortenberry. This is a complete failure to satisfy Requirement 4 and creates a material title defect. The recorded deed does not convey title to the intended buyer. A new, corrected deed must be executed and recorded immediately naming Kyla B. Fortenberry as the sole grantee. The relationship between the incorrectly named grantees and the intended buyer must be clarified, and the defective deed must be addressed (whether through correction, cancellation, or other appropriate remedy).

Requirement 5: Record Deed of Trust
  • Description: Record satisfactory executed Deed of Trust from KYLA B. FORTENBERRY as Trustor, in favor of SOCIETY MORTGAGE, as Beneficiary, securing an original principal balance of $80,808.00.
  • Status: OPEN - PENDING CORRECTED DEED
  • Analysis: The deed of trust cannot be recorded until the owner's deed is corrected and Kyla B. Fortenberry has clear record title to the property. This requirement is dependent upon satisfaction of Requirement 4.

Requirement 6: LLC Documentation and Compliance
  • Description: Submit: (a) Copy of operating agreement and amendments or certification of none; (b) Evidence LLC is in good standing; (c) Resolution signed by all members authorizing transaction; (d) Other requirements following review of organizational documents.
  • Status: OPEN - INCOMPLETE DOCUMENTATION
  • Analysis: Complete LLC compliance documentation has not been submitted. The following must be provided:
    (a) Certified copy of the Operating Agreement for GARNER TREE FARM, LLC and any amendments, or a certification that no operating agreement exists;
    (b) Certificate of Good Standing from the Mississippi Secretary of State, dated within 30 days of closing;
    (c) Certified resolution signed by all four members (Ricky Lane McPhail, Terri Garner Crawley, Patsy Garner Brewton, and Karen Garner Delancey) authorizing the sale of the property and the execution of all transaction documents;
    (d) Any additional documentation required following review of the organizational documents, including member consent forms if required by the operating agreement.
    
    This documentation is essential to establish the LLC's authority to convey the property and to confirm that all members have consented to the transaction.
